commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
Adds a Mattermost thread-fetch helper, seeds an empty in-memory thread history from the server before inbound context assembly, and adds unit coverage for the backfill behavior.

PR surface: Source +86, Tests +111. Total +197 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main keeps Mattermost thread context in a process-local history map with no server-thread fetch, so restart or session clear can leave the inbound turn with no prior thread messages.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow PR-branch repair can make the backfill trigger recovery-aware and add focused regression coverage for active-thread steady state.

target.title: fix(mattermost): backfill thread history from server when in-memory window is empty
target.body:
## Summary
- Thread/conversation history is in-memory only, so after a gateway restart or a session clear the window for a thread is empty. A user who @mentions the bot inside an existing thread to wake it then gets a reply with no thread context — worst exactly when the bot was unresponsive and is being recovered.
- Add `fetchMattermostThread` (`GET /posts/{id}/thread`) and `backfillMattermostThreadHistory`, which seeds the history window from the server thread (resolving usernames, trimmed to `historyLimit`) when the in-memory window is empty.
- Wired into the inbound path for thread messages (`threadRootId` present and a history key); failures are logged and swallowed so inbound handling continues.
- Add unit coverage (seed from server, current-post exclusion + trim, no-overwrite when non-empty, error swallow).

Fixes #93204. Part of the #65729 umbrella split.

## Scope
- Applies to any thread that has a history key. With current behavior DMs have a null history key, so this affects group/channel threads; DM coverage would follow the separate DM-threading track (#93203).
- No new config. Backfill only runs when the window is empty, so steady-state behavior is unchanged.

## Real behavior proof
Live Mattermost channel thread, bot `@openclaw`, on a deployment running the equivalent change. After clearing the session mid-thread (`/new`), a follow-up is still answered from thread context, because the empty in-memory window is backfilled from the server thread:

![thread context survives session clear](https://github.com/iloveleon19/openclaw/releases/download/pr93243-proof/pr93243-proof-1.png)

- `leon: @openclaw 我昨天看到一台黃色的車子` (yesterday I saw a yellow car).
- `leon: @openclaw /new` → `openclaw: 新的話題開始。` (session/history cleared).
- `leon: @openclaw 我昨天見到什麼?` (what did I see yesterday?).
- `openclaw: 你昨天看到一台黃色的車子。` — recalled from the server thread, re-fetched because the in-memory window was empty after the clear.

## Tests
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.test.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/client.test.ts` — 81 passed
- `corepack pnpm format:check extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/client.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.test.ts`
target.author: iloveleon19

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
